acceptance.proof: Attempts and state storage are explicitly bounded; ordered Attempting/Retryable/Recovered/Terminal/Exhausted/Cancelled/Deadline outcomes are observable without background work.
A caller-labelled transient failure followed by one accepted timestamped Float32 sample composes into and out of the accepted bounded queue with exact bits.
No endpoint selection, automatic reconnection, replay, hidden retry defaults, worker, async runtime, device, unsafe/FFI, or Manifold authority is added.

commit_policy: Commit the finite recovery coordinator, state trace, queue composition, tests, and routing coherently before Standard validation; keep lifecycle and push commits intentional.

instruction_surfaces.change_reason: Route finite recovery states and retain caller authority.
Describe only explicit synchronous finite recovery.
Document attempt ownership, state bounds, deadline, and cancellation.
Route success, terminal, exhaustion, cancellation, deadline, and state-bound cases.
Review finite state and caller authority; no shared mutation expected.
Review Rusty LSL lane and public/private boundaries; no shared mutation expected.

non_scope: Automatic endpoint selection or reconnection, packet replay, queue replacement, additional sample formats, clock policy, persistence, backoff algorithms, jitter, performance claims, or default retry counts.
Owned threads, async runtime, timers beyond explicit blocking delay/deadline, global state, devices, unsafe, FFI, dependencies, or official implementation source.
Manifold admission, registry, session, route, topology, lease, authorization, replay, or audit authority.
objective: Add one dependency-free caller-invoked finite recovery coordinator for the accepted timestamped Float32 queue path: explicit nonzero attempt/state bounds, caller-labelled retryable versus terminal failures, bounded delay/deadline, cancellation, and an observable ordered state trace without owned workers or hidden reconnection authority.
outputs: One explicit finite recovery policy with nonzero maximum attempts, state bound, delay slice, and total deadline.
One synchronous caller-supplied attempt interface whose retryable/terminal classification remains caller evidence and whose ordered states are returned.
Tests for transient success into the accepted queue, terminal failure, exhaustion, cancellation, deadline, state bound, and exact sample bits.
